title: tetraglochin paucijugata
intro: Tetraglochin paucijugata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and potentially lowering blood pressure. Traditional herbal preparations of Tetraglochin paucijugata include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are typically used to treat a range of ailments.
